adapter_limitations: LiteLLM exposes blocked status but not our economic exclusion reason; the adapter preserves the reason separately.
LiteLLM cost-based routing sums configured input and output unit prices; fixtures split the scalar quote equally across them.
No model request is sent, so fallback execution and service-system fidelity remain outside this experiment.
claim_boundary: This validates LiteLLM 1.92.0 deployment filtering, weighted selection, sequential exclusion, and scalar lowest-cost selection on synthetic fixtures. Inverse-price scores are explicitly mapped into LiteLLM weights; this is not evidence that LiteLLM or OpenRouter natively uses inverse-price routing, and it does not validate live production state, queueing, latency, or provider conduct.
interval_method: Clopper-Pearson intervals with Bonferroni alpha=0.05/family_cells; simultaneous over every first-route share and fallback permutation cell
